skill.name: linear-automation
skill.description: Automate Linear project management tasks through Rube MCP using Composio's Linear toolkit for issues, projects, cycles, teams, and labels.
skill.summary: Automate Linear project management via Rube MCP with tool orchestration for issues, projects, cycles, and team workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 63 static analyzer findings dismissed as false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation-only (184 lines) describing Rube MCP tool orchestration for Linear API. No executable code, command injection vectors, or dangerous patterns exist. External commands detected were markdown-formatted tool names (e.g., LINEAR_GET_ALL_LINEAR_TEAMS), not shell execution. The hardcoded URL is the legitimate Rube MCP endpoint for user configuration.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: Static scanner matched markdown backtick formatting around tool names (e.g., LINEAR_GET_ALL_LINEAR_TEAMS) as shell execution. No actual shell or Ruby backtick execution exists in this documentation file.
The URL at line 22 (https://rube.app/mcp) is the documented MCP server endpoint for user configuration, not an unauthorized network call. No data exfiltration or suspicious network activity.

content.value_statement: Eliminate manual Linear operations by orchestrating MCP tools for issue tracking, project management, and sprint cycles. Focus on work instead of repetitive task management.
actual_capabilities: Create, search, update, and list Linear issues with proper team and state resolution
Manage Linear projects including creation, updates, and team associations
Work with Linear cycles (sprints) scoped by team with proper ID resolution
Create labels and comments on issues with Markdown support
Execute custom GraphQL queries against Linear API for advanced operations
Resolve entity IDs (teams, states, users) before performing operations
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server to be configured and connected to Linear
Team-specific operations require valid team ID resolution before execution
Custom GraphQL queries require knowledge of Linear's GraphQL schema
Subject to Linear API rate limits for GraphQL and REST operations

use_cases.title: Engineering Team Lead
use_cases.description: Automate sprint planning by creating issues from a template, assigning team members, and setting appropriate workflow states without manual Linear navigation.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering managers and team leads managing multiple team members

use_cases.title: Product Manager
use_cases.description: Bulk create and organize project issues, link them to cycles, and apply consistent labeling across teams for better roadmap visibility.

use_cases.title: Developer Automation
use_cases.description: Create issues automatically from code review comments, link commits to issues, and update issue states based on pull request status.
use_cases.target_user: Developers integrating Linear with their development workflow

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Create a Linear Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Linear issue with title "Fix login bug" for the Engineering team. Set priority to high and add a description explaining the bug occurs on Safari browsers.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Search and Update Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all issues in the Engineering team with "bug" in the title that are currently unassigned. Update each one to assign to the team lead and set priority to medium.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Create Project with Multiple Teams
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Linear project called "Q1 Mobile Redesign" spanning the Engineering, Design, and Product teams. Add a description with project goals and create an initial issue for the kickoff meeting.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Custom GraphQL Query for Cycle Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a custom GraphQL query to get all completed issues in the current cycle for the Engineering team. Calculate the total story points completed and list issues by assignee.

output_examples.input: Create an issue titled "API rate limiting" for the backend team with high priority
output_examples.output: Successfully created issue ENG-1247 "API rate limiting" with high priority assigned to the backend team. Issue is now in Todo state. You can view it at linear.app/issue/ENG-1247

output_examples.input: List all active cycles for the Engineering team
output_examples.output: Found 2 active cycles for Engineering team: Cycle 24 (Jan 15 - Jan 28) with 18 issues, Cycle 25 (Jan 29 - Feb 11) with 22 issues. Current active cycle is Cycle 25.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Resolve team IDs and state IDs using the appropriate lookup tools before creating issues or cycles
Check pagination cursors in responses and iterate through all pages when listing large collections
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode team IDs or state IDs - always resolve them dynamically using lookup tools
Avoid creating issues without first verifying the team exists and the connection is ACTIVE
Do not skip error handling for API rate limits - implement retry logic with exponential backoff

faq.question: How do I set up the Rube MCP connection for Linear?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client configuration. Then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'linear' and follow the OAuth link to authorize Linear access.

faq.question: Why do I need to resolve team IDs before creating issues?
faq.answer: Linear issues are team-scoped entities. The API requires a valid team_id when creating issues. Use LINEAR_GET_ALL_LINEAR_TEAMS to list teams and find the correct ID.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without a Linear subscription?
faq.answer: No, you need an active Linear account with API access. The skill uses Linear's API through Rube MCP, which requires valid Linear credentials.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination when listing many issues?
faq.answer: Linear API returns paginated results. Check the response for cursor information and pass the cursor to subsequent requests until all pages are retrieved.

faq.question: What priority values should I use when creating issues?
faq.answer: Linear uses integer priority values: 0 (no priority), 1 (urgent), 2 (high), 3 (medium), 4 (low). Always use integers, not string names.

faq.question: Can I execute custom GraphQL queries with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, use LINEAR_RUN_QUERY_OR_MUTATION to execute custom GraphQL queries. You need knowledge of Linear's GraphQL schema and should be aware of rate limits.
